rationale.R1.2: The section contains a severe internal logical inconsistency: the aggregate calibration gap worsens under SelfCheck (0.74−0.61=0.13 baseline vs. 0.86−0.65=0.21 SelfCheck), yet the text concludes improved calibration. Three of five per-category gaps also widen. Whether the task is writing polish or critical review, this contradiction is structurally present and materially relevant.
rationale.R1.3: The headline claim — 'SelfCheck uniformly reduces overconfidence' — is directly contradicted by the reported numbers: code-execution (+0.10), file-read (+0.08), and database-query (+0.08) gaps all grow; the aggregate gap grows. The dismissal of three out of five worsened categories as 'noise' is unsupported. Evidence-claim alignment is load-bearing if review is requested, and residually relevant even for a polish task.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation 'help me with this section' is a canonical writing-assistance request; sentence and paragraph quality is therefore centrally in scope regardless of whether critical review is also intended.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on improving prose clarity and flow (writing polish only), or should I identify and correct the logical and statistical inconsistencies in the claims — specifically that the aggregate calibration gap widens under SelfCheck and three of five per-category gaps also worsen, contradicting the stated conclusion of uniform improvement?
